---
title: Rugby RSS Feed Implementation Plan
type: reference
tags: [rugby, rss, rsshub, plan, implementation]
created: 2026-04-14
status: active
source_task: "[[RUGBY_COACH_TASKS]]"

# Rugby RSS Feed Implementation Plan

This plan assumes "install rssfeed" means adding a durable RSS monitoring layer for rugby coaching accounts using the existing rugby dashboard, the installed Obsidian `rss-dashboard` plugin, and RSSHub as the feed generator.

## Current State Review

The live rugby implementation is ahead of `[[RUGBY_COACH_TASKS]]` in several areas:

- `scripts/dashboard/rugby-web.mjs` already has a Coaches tab, `RSSHUB_URL` support, account aggregation, per-account RSS links, and OPML export.
- `Rugby/Coaching Accounts.md` already documents RSSHub quick setup and includes an OPML bundle.
- The Obsidian `rss-dashboard` plugin is already installed in this vault.
- The plugin has no feeds configured yet, so RSS is not operational.

There are also three gaps that need to be fixed before bulk feed installation:

1. The dashboard and account note currently generate RSS URLs as `/instagram/user/<username>`.
2. That route is the RSSHub private-API Instagram route and requires `IG_USERNAME` and `IG_PASSWORD`.
3. Username extraction is not reliable yet. Some generated handles are clearly wrong in `Rugby/Coaching Accounts.md`, which would make bulk feed installs noisy and brittle.

## Recommended Architecture

Use a self-hosted RSSHub instance as the feed backend, then wire the rugby pipeline to generate and install feed definitions into the already-installed Obsidian plugin.

### Default Route Choice

Recommended default:

- `http://localhost:1200/picnob.info/user/<username>`

Fallback options:

- `http://localhost:1200/picnob/user/<username>`
- `http://localhost:1200/instagram/2/user/<username>`
- `http://localhost:1200/instagram/user/<username>` only if you are willing to store Instagram credentials on the RSSHub server

Reasoning:

- RSSHub documents `picnob.info/user/:id` and `picnob/user/:id` as public Instagram-profile routes.
- RSSHub documents `/instagram/user/:key` as the private-API route that requires server-side `IG_USERNAME` and `IG_PASSWORD`.
- The current rugby dashboard hardcodes the private route, so route selection needs to become configurable instead of embedded in the UI.

## Success Criteria

The RSS rollout is complete when all of the following are true:

- A local RSSHub instance is running and reachable from the same machine.
- At least 10 high-value coaching accounts resolve to valid RSS feeds.
- `node run.mjs rugby web` shows correct RSS links in the Coaches tab.
- The Obsidian `rss-dashboard` plugin contains installed feeds instead of an empty `feeds` array.
- Feed installation is reproducible from project data rather than manual point-and-click setup.

## Implementation Phases

## Phase 1 - Install and Verify RSSHub

Objective: get a local feed service running before changing any rugby code.

Tasks:

- Install Docker Desktop if it is not already available.
- Start RSSHub locally on port `1200`.
- Add `RSSHUB_URL=http://localhost:1200` to `.env`.
- Verify 3 sample coach feeds manually in a browser before automating anything.

Recommended first-pass command:

```bash
docker run -d --name rsshub -p 1200:1200 diygod/rsshub
```

Validation:

- `http://localhost:1200/picnob.info/user/craigmcleodrugby`
- `http://localhost:1200/picnob.info/user/riseuprugby`
- `http://localhost:1200/picnob.info/user/rodge_academy`

Exit criteria:

- At least 2 of 3 sample feeds return RSS XML.

## Phase 2 - Fix Account Identity Quality

Objective: stop bad usernames from polluting feed installs.

Problems seen in current data:

- Some accounts are mapped to obviously wrong handles.
- Account usernames are inferred too loosely inside the dashboard layer.
- There is no stable override file for manual corrections.

Planned changes:

- Create a small rugby RSS helper module, for example `scripts/lib/rugby-rss.mjs`.
- Move account extraction logic out of `scripts/dashboard/rugby-web.mjs` into that helper.
- Prefer `title: "Video by <handle>"` when present, but do not trust it blindly.
- Add a manual override file such as `Rugby/rss/account-overrides.json`.
- Regenerate `Rugby/Coaching Accounts.md` from normalized account data instead of ad hoc UI logic.

Validation:

- Review the top 25 accounts by post count.
- Confirm username accuracy for all accounts that will be installed into RSS.

Exit criteria:

- No obvious false-handle mappings remain in the top account list.

## Phase 3 - Normalize RSS URL Construction

Objective: make route selection explicit and changeable.

Planned changes:

- Add a helper that builds feed URLs from:
  - `RSSHUB_URL`
  - `RUGBY_RSS_ROUTE`
  - normalized account username
- Support route values such as:
  - `picnob.info`
  - `picnob`
  - `instagram2`
  - `instagram-private`
- Replace hardcoded `/instagram/user/` URLs in:
  - `scripts/dashboard/rugby-web.mjs`
  - any generator used for `Rugby/Coaching Accounts.md`
  - OPML export output

Secondary cleanup:

- Replace the current browser-side `no-cors` reachability check with a server-side probe endpoint, because the current check cannot distinguish a reachable server from a blocked or opaque response reliably.

Validation:

- Start `node run.mjs rugby web`.
- Open the Coaches tab.
- Confirm each displayed RSS link matches the configured route style.
- Download OPML and inspect several generated URLs.

Exit criteria:

- The route can be changed with env/config only, without touching UI code.

## Phase 4 - Install Feeds Into Obsidian RSS Dashboard

Objective: turn the installed plugin into an actual operating surface.

Important caution:

- `.obsidian/plugins/rss-dashboard/data.json` currently shows global settings and folders, but no sample feed objects.
- Do not guess the feed object schema and bulk-write it blind.

Safe rollout order:

1. Add one feed manually in the Obsidian plugin UI.
2. Inspect the resulting `data.json` entry shape.
3. Capture that schema in code.
4. Automate feed installation only after the schema is confirmed from the live plugin.

Planned automation:

- Add a command such as `node run.mjs rugby rss-install`.
- The command should:
  - back up `.obsidian/plugins/rss-dashboard/data.json`
  - generate a deterministic feed list from normalized account data
  - create or reuse a rugby-specific folder in the plugin config
  - insert missing feeds
  - avoid duplicating feeds on repeated runs

Nice-to-have companion commands:

- `node run.mjs rugby rss-status`
- `node run.mjs rugby rss-export`

Validation:

- Open Obsidian.
- Confirm feeds appear under the expected folder.
- Refresh feeds.
- Confirm recent posts load for the installed coaches.

Exit criteria:

- Re-running the installer is idempotent and does not duplicate entries.

## Phase 5 - Connect RSS Back to the Rugby Pipeline

Objective: use feeds to reduce manual maintenance of `Rugby/Rugby Links.md`.

This phase is optional, but it is where RSS becomes operationally valuable.

Possible flow:

- Pull latest feed items from installed coach feeds.
- Extract Instagram post URLs from feed items.
- Compare against `.cache/rugby/*.json` and `Rugby/Rugby Links.md`.
- Write unseen URLs into a rugby inbox file or append them to `Rugby/Rugby Links.md`.
- Reuse the existing pipeline:
  - `node run.mjs rugby extract`
  - `node run.mjs rugby classify`
  - `node run.mjs rugby ingest`

Recommended first version:

- Do not auto-append into `Rugby/Rugby Links.md` immediately.
- Write candidates to a review file first, then promote approved links into the main source file.

Exit criteria:

- New coaching content can be surfaced weekly without manual account-by-account browsing.

## File-Level Plan

Likely files to change during implementation:

- `scripts/dashboard/rugby-web.mjs`
- `scripts/cmd/router.mjs`
- `scripts/lib/rugby-rss.mjs` new
- `scripts/pullers/rugby-rss-install.mjs` new
- `Rugby/Coaching Accounts.md`
- `Rugby/rss/account-overrides.json` new
- `.obsidian/plugins/rss-dashboard/data.json`
- `.env`

## Risks and Controls

### Instagram Anti-Crawling

Risk:

- Some RSSHub Instagram routes are fragile and may degrade on public instances.

Control:

- Prefer a local RSSHub instance.
- Keep route selection configurable.
- Test with a small coach set before bulk install.

### Bad Username Mapping

Risk:

- Incorrect handles will create dead feeds and noisy plugin state.

Control:

- Add an override file.
- Validate the top accounts before installation.

### Plugin Schema Drift

Risk:

- Writing directly into `rss-dashboard` config without first observing a real feed entry could break plugin state.

Control:

- Seed one feed manually and inspect the resulting JSON before scripting.
- Always create a timestamped backup before automated writes.

## Recommended Implementation Order

1. Install RSSHub locally and validate 3 sample feeds.
2. Normalize account usernames and add an override file.
3. Replace hardcoded RSS route generation with a shared helper.
4. Manually add 1 feed in Obsidian and inspect plugin JSON schema.
5. Build `rugby rss-install` to populate feeds safely.
6. Add `rugby rss-status` and OPML export regeneration if needed.
7. Optionally bridge RSS-discovered URLs back into the rugby ingestion pipeline.

## Definition of Done

The RSS work should be considered done only when:

- feed generation works from local config,
- feed links in the rugby dashboard are correct,
- Obsidian has installed feeds,
- repeated installs are safe,
- and the system can be maintained without editing URLs by hand in multiple places.

content.newText:
---
title: Rugby Practice Builder Implementation Plan
type: reference
tags: [rugby, dashboard, practice-planning, video-management, implementation]
created: 2026-04-14
status: active
source_context: "[[RUGBY_COACH_TASKS]]"
---

# Rugby Practice Builder Implementation Plan

This plan covers two dashboard-side features for the rugby project:

1. practice templates and session planning for coaches
2. safe video edit and remove flows inside the web dashboard

Not in scope:

- Obsidian RSS plugin integration
- Google Drive file deletion automation in the first pass
- mobile apps or separate frontend build tooling

## Current State

The current rugby dashboard already provides:

- drill ingestion from `Rugby/Posts/*.md`
- frontmatter parsing inside `scripts/dashboard/rugby-web.mjs`
- filtering by age group, category, difficulty, and search
- inline Google Drive video embeds from `gdrive_url`

The current drill note schema already gives a usable base:

```yaml
type: rugby_drill
post_id: C0PuScQoTeV
skill_category: "Flag Rugby"
sub_skill: "Tag - Remove"
coaching_cue: "One of my go toos"
age_groups: ["Youth","Junior","Senior"]
difficulty: intermediate
gdrive_url: "https://drive.google.com/open?id=..."
```

That means the practice planner can be built without changing the ingest pipeline first. The dashboard can treat each drill note as a planning building block.

## Product Goals

### Practice Templates

Allow a coach to:

- browse drills on a tablet
- add drills into a draft practice
- organize the practice by block
- save reusable templates
- duplicate a template into a dated session plan
- print or export the plan cleanly

### Video Management

Allow a coach or admin to:

- replace a broken or outdated `gdrive_url`
- clear a video from the dashboard without deleting the drill note
- hide known-bad videos from the dashboard until fixed

## Recommended Data Model

## 1. Practice Templates

Create a new folder:

- `Rugby/Practice Templates/`

Each template is a markdown note with frontmatter and a human-readable body.

Recommended frontmatter:

```yaml
type: rugby_practice_template
title: "Youth Defence Pressure"
age_group: "Youth"
duration_minutes: 75
theme: "Defence"
difficulty: "intermediate"
equipment: ["balls", "cones", "hit shields"]
objectives:
  - "Improve first-up line speed"
  - "Build tackle and reload habits"
template_blocks:
  - label: "Warm-up"
    minutes: 10
    drills: ["[[C0PuScQoTeV]]"]
  - label: "Skill Block"
    minutes: 20
    drills: ["[[C1T3k2PIqVq]]"]
  - label: "Game Block"
    minutes: 25
    drills: ["[[C28DEcPOHrt]]"]
  - label: "Conditioning"
    minutes: 10
    drills: []
  - label: "Review"
    minutes: 10
    drills: []
tags: [rugby, practice-template]
```

Recommendation:

- keep drill references in frontmatter as wikilinks to existing drill notes
- keep the body as a printable coach sheet with setup notes, regressions, and coaching emphasis

## 2. Practice Sessions

Create a second folder:

- `Rugby/Practice Sessions/`

Sessions are concrete dated plans derived from templates.

Recommended frontmatter:

```yaml
type: rugby_practice_session
title: "2026-04-18 Youth Defence Pressure"
date: 2026-04-18
team: "U14"
age_group: "Youth"
duration_minutes: 75
source_template: "[[Youth Defence Pressure]]"
status: draft
session_blocks:
  - label: "Warm-up"
    minutes: 10
    drills: ["[[C0PuScQoTeV]]"]
weather_notes: ""
attendance_notes: ""
tags: [rugby, practice-session]
```

Why split templates from sessions:

- templates stay reusable and clean
- sessions become a dated coaching record
- the dashboard can offer “Use Template” and “Save as Session” separately

## 3. Video State

Do not treat missing video and intentionally hidden video as the same thing.

Extend drill-note frontmatter with:

```yaml
gdrive_url: ""
video_status: available
video_updated_at: 2026-04-14
video_notes: ""
```

Recommended allowed values:

- `available`
- `missing`
- `hidden`
- `broken`

Behavior:

- `available`: embed and show Watch badge
- `missing`: no embed, normal informational message
- `hidden`: no embed and omit from video-focused views
- `broken`: no embed, show “video needs repair”

## Dashboard UX Plan

## 1. New Tabs

Add two tabs to the existing rugby dashboard:

- `Planner`
- `Manage`

Suggested tab responsibilities:

- `Drills`: existing browsing and filtering surface
- `Glossary`: existing taxonomy surface
- `Coaches`: existing account surface
- `Planner`: build templates and sessions
- `Manage`: edit drill metadata and video links

## 2. Planner Flow

### Planner Sidebar

Show:

- active draft practice
- total minutes
- block list
- selected age group
- selected theme
- save buttons

### Drill Card Actions

Add flat actions on each card:

- `Add to Practice`
- `Preview Video`
- `Edit`

### Practice Builder Interaction

The coach should be able to:

- create a blank draft
- choose template or session mode
- add a drill into a selected block
- move drills up and down
- rename blocks
- add or remove blocks
- edit minutes per block
- print the resulting plan

Recommended first-pass block types:

- Warm-up
- Skill
- Unit
- Game
- Conditioning
- Review

No drag-and-drop is required for MVP. Up/down controls are enough.

## 3. Manage Flow

Each drill opens an edit panel with:

- editable `coaching_cue`
- editable `difficulty`
- editable `age_groups`
- editable `gdrive_url`
- editable `video_status`
- save and cancel actions

Video-specific actions:

- `Replace Video Link`
- `Remove Video From Dashboard`
- `Mark Video Broken`
- `Hide Video`

For MVP:

- “Remove” means clear `gdrive_url` and set `video_status: missing`
- do not delete the Google Drive file

## API Plan

Extend `scripts/dashboard/rugby-web.mjs` with write endpoints.

## Read Endpoints

- `GET /api/drills`
- `GET /api/glossary`
- `GET /api/accounts`

Add:

- `GET /api/templates`
- `GET /api/sessions`
- `GET /api/drills/:id`

## Write Endpoints

Add:

- `POST /api/templates`
- `PATCH /api/templates/:id`
- `POST /api/sessions`
- `PATCH /api/sessions/:id`
- `PATCH /api/drills/:id`
- `PATCH /api/drills/:id/video`
- `DELETE /api/drills/:id/video`

Recommended semantics:

- `PATCH /api/drills/:id` handles normal drill metadata edits
- `PATCH /api/drills/:id/video` updates only `gdrive_url`, `video_status`, and `video_notes`
- `DELETE /api/drills/:id/video` clears `gdrive_url` and sets `video_status: missing`

## Persistence Plan

Use markdown files as the source of truth.

Why:

- consistent with the rest of the vault
- editable in Obsidian
- easy to back up and diff
- no extra database

Implementation approach:

- continue parsing frontmatter on startup
- add write helpers for reading a note, patching frontmatter, and writing it back
- watch `Rugby/Practice Templates/`, `Rugby/Practice Sessions/`, and `Rugby/Posts/` for hot reload

Recommended helper additions:

- `scripts/lib/rugby-practice.mjs`
- `scripts/lib/rugby-note-patch.mjs`

Responsibilities:

- template/session note generation
- block serialization
- safe frontmatter patching
- slug generation for template and session filenames

## File-Level Change Plan

Primary files:

- `scripts/dashboard/rugby-web.mjs`
- `scripts/cmd/router.mjs`

New files:

- `scripts/lib/rugby-practice.mjs`
- `scripts/lib/rugby-note-patch.mjs`
- `Rugby/Practice Templates/` folder
- `Rugby/Practice Sessions/` folder

Optional support note:

- `Rugby/Practice Builder Implementation Plan.md`

## Recommended Delivery Phases

## Phase A - Template Persistence

Build the note model first.

Tasks:

- create template and session folders
- define frontmatter shape
- add read/write helpers
- add `GET /api/templates` and `POST /api/templates`

Exit criteria:

- a template can be created and saved as a markdown note

## Phase B - Planner UI MVP

Build the coach workflow next.

Tasks:

- add `Planner` tab
- add draft practice state in browser
- add `Add to Practice` on drill cards
- add block editing controls
- add `Save Template`
- add print stylesheet for plans

Exit criteria:

- a coach can build and save a reusable template from the dashboard

## Phase C - Session Generation

Add dated practice plans.

Tasks:

- add `Create Session From Template`
- add session note writing
- add `GET /api/sessions`
- add session status fields

Exit criteria:

- a coach can duplicate a template into a dated session note

## Phase D - Video Management

Add safe metadata editing.

Tasks:

- add `Manage` tab or slide-over editor
- add drill metadata patch endpoint
- add video patch/delete endpoints
- add `video_status` support in drill rendering

Exit criteria:

- a coach can replace a link, clear a link, and mark a video broken without leaving the dashboard

## Phase E - Quality Pass

Tasks:

- improve mobile/tablet spacing
- add unsaved-change warnings
- add success and error toast messages
- add confirmation modal for destructive actions
- verify print output

## Recommended MVP Scope

If you want the fastest useful version, build only this:

1. template notes in `Rugby/Practice Templates/`
2. `Planner` tab with add/remove/reorder blocks
3. `Save Template`
4. `PATCH /api/drills/:id/video`
5. `DELETE /api/drills/:id/video`
6. `video_status` support in card rendering
7. print-friendly practice template output

Defer these until later:

- drag-and-drop
- Google Drive deletion
- multi-user auth
- attendance tracking
- session analytics

## Risks and Controls

### Frontmatter Corruption

Risk:

- ad hoc string replacement can damage drill notes

Control:

- centralize note patching in one helper
- always preserve non-target fields
- write backups before first rollout if needed

### UI Complexity

Risk:

Risk:

- coaches may expect “remove” to mean “hide”, not “delete forever”

Control:

- do not delete Drive files in the dashboard

- coaches can save reusable practice templates from the dashboard

# Rugby Practice Builder Implementation Plan

This plan covers two dashboard-side features for the rugby project:

1. practice templates and session planning for coaches
2. safe video edit and remove flows inside the web dashboard

Not in scope:

- Obsidian RSS plugin integration
- Google Drive file deletion automation in the first pass
- mobile apps or separate frontend build tooling

## Current State

The current rugby dashboard already provides:

- drill ingestion from `Rugby/Posts/*.md`
- frontmatter parsing inside `scripts/dashboard/rugby-web.mjs`
- filtering by age group, category, difficulty, and search
- inline Google Drive video embeds from `gdrive_url`

The current drill note schema already gives a usable base:

```yaml
type: rugby_drill
post_id: C0PuScQoTeV
skill_category: "Flag Rugby"
sub_skill: "Tag - Remove"
coaching_cue: "One of my go toos"
age_groups: ["Youth","Junior","Senior"]
difficulty: intermediate
gdrive_url: "https://drive.google.com/open?id=..."
```

That means the practice planner can be built without changing the ingest pipeline first. The dashboard can treat each drill note as a planning building block.

## Product Goals

### Practice Templates

Allow a coach to:

- browse drills on a tablet
- add drills into a draft practice
- organize the practice by block
- save reusable templates
- duplicate a template into a dated session plan
- print or export the plan cleanly

### Video Management

Allow a coach or admin to:

- replace a broken or outdated `gdrive_url`
- clear a video from the dashboard without deleting the drill note
- hide known-bad videos from the dashboard until fixed

## Recommended Data Model

## 1. Practice Templates

Create a new folder:

- `Rugby/Practice Templates/`

Each template is a markdown note with frontmatter and a human-readable body.

Recommended frontmatter:

```yaml
type: rugby_practice_template
title: "Youth Defence Pressure"
age_group: "Youth"
duration_minutes: 75
theme: "Defence"
difficulty: "intermediate"
equipment: ["balls", "cones", "hit shields"]
objectives:
  - "Improve first-up line speed"
  - "Build tackle and reload habits"
template_blocks:
  - label: "Warm-up"
    minutes: 10
    drills: ["[[C0PuScQoTeV]]"]
  - label: "Skill Block"
    minutes: 20
    drills: ["[[C1T3k2PIqVq]]"]
  - label: "Game Block"
    minutes: 25
    drills: ["[[C28DEcPOHrt]]"]
  - label: "Conditioning"
    minutes: 10
    drills: []
  - label: "Review"
    minutes: 10
    drills: []
tags: [rugby, practice-template]
```

Recommendation:

- keep drill references in frontmatter as wikilinks to existing drill notes
- keep the body as a printable coach sheet with setup notes, regressions, and coaching emphasis

## 2. Practice Sessions

Create a second folder:

- `Rugby/Practice Sessions/`

Sessions are concrete dated plans derived from templates.

Recommended frontmatter:

```yaml
type: rugby_practice_session
title: "2026-04-18 Youth Defence Pressure"
date: 2026-04-18
team: "U14"
age_group: "Youth"
duration_minutes: 75
source_template: "[[Youth Defence Pressure]]"
status: draft
session_blocks:
  - label: "Warm-up"
    minutes: 10
    drills: ["[[C0PuScQoTeV]]"]
weather_notes: ""
attendance_notes: ""
tags: [rugby, practice-session]
```

Why split templates from sessions:

- templates stay reusable and clean
- sessions become a dated coaching record
- the dashboard can offer “Use Template” and “Save as Session” separately

## 3. Video State

Do not treat missing video and intentionally hidden video as the same thing.

Extend drill-note frontmatter with:

```yaml
gdrive_url: ""
video_status: available
video_updated_at: 2026-04-14
video_notes: ""
```

Recommended allowed values:

- `available`
- `missing`
- `hidden`
- `broken`

Behavior:

- `available`: embed and show Watch badge
- `missing`: no embed, normal informational message
- `hidden`: no embed and omit from video-focused views
- `broken`: no embed, show “video needs repair”

## Dashboard UX Plan

## 1. New Tabs

Add two tabs to the existing rugby dashboard:

- `Planner`
- `Manage`

Suggested tab responsibilities:

- `Drills`: existing browsing and filtering surface
- `Glossary`: existing taxonomy surface
- `Coaches`: existing account surface
- `Planner`: build templates and sessions
- `Manage`: edit drill metadata and video links

## 2. Planner Flow

### Planner Sidebar

Show:

- active draft practice
- total minutes
- block list
- selected age group
- selected theme
- save buttons

### Drill Card Actions

Add flat actions on each card:

- `Add to Practice`
- `Preview Video`
- `Edit`

### Practice Builder Interaction

The coach should be able to:

- create a blank draft
- choose template or session mode
- add a drill into a selected block
- move drills up and down
- rename blocks
- add or remove blocks
- edit minutes per block
- print the resulting plan

Recommended first-pass block types:

- Warm-up
- Skill
- Unit
- Game
- Conditioning
- Review

No drag-and-drop is required for MVP. Up/down controls are enough.

## 3. Manage Flow

Each drill opens an edit panel with:

- editable `coaching_cue`
- editable `difficulty`
- editable `age_groups`
- editable `gdrive_url`
- editable `video_status`
- save and cancel actions

Video-specific actions:

- `Replace Video Link`
- `Remove Video From Dashboard`
- `Mark Video Broken`
- `Hide Video`

For MVP:

- “Remove” means clear `gdrive_url` and set `video_status: missing`
- do not delete the Google Drive file

## API Plan

Extend `scripts/dashboard/rugby-web.mjs` with write endpoints.

## Read Endpoints

- `GET /api/drills`
- `GET /api/glossary`
- `GET /api/accounts`

Add:

- `GET /api/templates`
- `GET /api/sessions`
- `GET /api/drills/:id`

## Write Endpoints

Add:

- `POST /api/templates`
- `PATCH /api/templates/:id`
- `POST /api/sessions`
- `PATCH /api/sessions/:id`
- `PATCH /api/drills/:id`
- `PATCH /api/drills/:id/video`
- `DELETE /api/drills/:id/video`

Recommended semantics:

- `PATCH /api/drills/:id` handles normal drill metadata edits
- `PATCH /api/drills/:id/video` updates only `gdrive_url`, `video_status`, and `video_notes`
- `DELETE /api/drills/:id/video` clears `gdrive_url` and sets `video_status: missing`

## Persistence Plan

Use markdown files as the source of truth.

Why:

- consistent with the rest of the vault
- editable in Obsidian
- easy to back up and diff
- no extra database

Implementation approach:

- continue parsing frontmatter on startup
- add write helpers for reading a note, patching frontmatter, and writing it back
- watch `Rugby/Practice Templates/`, `Rugby/Practice Sessions/`, and `Rugby/Posts/` for hot reload

Recommended helper additions:

- `scripts/lib/rugby-practice.mjs`
- `scripts/lib/rugby-note-patch.mjs`

Responsibilities:

- template/session note generation
- block serialization
- safe frontmatter patching
- slug generation for template and session filenames

## File-Level Change Plan

Primary files:

- `scripts/dashboard/rugby-web.mjs`
- `scripts/cmd/router.mjs`

New files:

- `scripts/lib/rugby-practice.mjs`
- `scripts/lib/rugby-note-patch.mjs`
- `Rugby/Practice Templates/` folder
- `Rugby/Practice Sessions/` folder

Optional support note:

- `Rugby/Practice Builder Implementation Plan.md`

## Recommended Delivery Phases

## Phase A - Template Persistence

Build the note model first.

Tasks:

- create template and session folders
- define frontmatter shape
- add read/write helpers
- add `GET /api/templates` and `POST /api/templates`

Exit criteria:

- a template can be created and saved as a markdown note

## Phase B - Planner UI MVP

Build the coach workflow next.

Tasks:

- add `Planner` tab
- add draft practice state in browser
- add `Add to Practice` on drill cards
- add block editing controls
- add `Save Template`
- add print stylesheet for plans

Exit criteria:

- a coach can build and save a reusable template from the dashboard

## Phase C - Session Generation

Add dated practice plans.

Tasks:

- add `Create Session From Template`
- add session note writing
- add `GET /api/sessions`
- add session status fields

Exit criteria:

- a coach can duplicate a template into a dated session note

## Phase D - Video Management

Add safe metadata editing.

Tasks:

- add `Manage` tab or slide-over editor
- add drill metadata patch endpoint
- add video patch/delete endpoints
- add `video_status` support in drill rendering

Exit criteria:

- a coach can replace a link, clear a link, and mark a video broken without leaving the dashboard

## Phase E - Quality Pass

Tasks:

- improve mobile/tablet spacing
- add unsaved-change warnings
- add success and error toast messages
- add confirmation modal for destructive actions
- verify print output

## Recommended MVP Scope

If you want the fastest useful version, build only this:

1. template notes in `Rugby/Practice Templates/`
2. `Planner` tab with add/remove/reorder blocks
3. `Save Template`
4. `PATCH /api/drills/:id/video`
5. `DELETE /api/drills/:id/video`
6. `video_status` support in card rendering
7. print-friendly practice template output

Defer these until later:

- drag-and-drop
- Google Drive deletion
- multi-user auth
- attendance tracking
- session analytics

## Risks and Controls

### Frontmatter Corruption

Risk:

- ad hoc string replacement can damage drill notes

Control:

- centralize note patching in one helper
- always preserve non-target fields
- write backups before first rollout if needed

### UI Complexity

Risk:

- adding planning, admin, and browsing into one file can turn `rugby-web.mjs` into a maintenance problem

Control:

- keep shared logic in helper modules
- separate data-loading, write endpoints, and HTML sections cleanly

### Unsafe Video Deletes

Risk:

- coaches may expect “remove” to mean “hide”, not “delete forever”

Control:

- MVP remove action only clears `gdrive_url` in the note
- do not delete Drive files in the dashboard

## Definition of Done

This feature set is done when:

- coaches can save reusable practice templates from the dashboard
- coaches can create dated session plans from those templates
- coaches can replace or clear video links safely
- broken or hidden videos render correctly in the UI
- all changes persist back into markdown notes inside the rugby vault

The next phase is implemented.

The planner is now a real draft workflow in [rugby-web.mjs](</C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/scripts/dashboard/rugby-web.mjs:1237>): coaches can edit blocks, set an active block, add drills from the `Drills` tab into that block, reorder/remove drills, load an existing template into the draft, save the draft back as a template, and save it as a dated session. Backend persistence for sessions is in [rugby-practice.mjs](</C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/scripts/lib/rugby-practice.mjs:87>), and I recorded the rollout in [Practice Builder Implementation Plan.md](</C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/Rugby/Practice Builder Implementation Plan.md:70>).

Verification passed for the code paths that matter:
- `node --check` passed for the dashboard and practice helper files.
- The inline browser script served by the dashboard was extracted and passed `node --check`.
- Live API smoke tests passed for template create, template overwrite, and session create.
- The disposable smoke template/session notes were removed afterward.
